authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR Part 484 - Home Health Services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal Conditions of Participation for Medicare-certified home health agencies. It directly supports the operating baseline for acceptance-to-service, clinical manager oversight, care coordination, QAPI, services under arrangement, and clinical records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: home service intake, acceptance-to-service, operational oversight, clinical-manager routing, and services-under-arrangement review
used_for: day-to-day operation and clinical manager boundary

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 484.60 - Condition of participation: Care planning, coordination of services, and quality of care
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal home health care-planning and coordination regulation. It grounds plan-of-care, orders, service coordination, visit schedule, written instructions, and clinical-manager dependencies.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: plan-of-care prerequisite validation, order handling, visit schedule confirmation, care coordination, and clinical escalation
used_for: visit schedule and patient instruction evidence

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 484.110 - Condition of participation: Clinical records
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal home health clinical-record regulation. It anchors the clinical record as the source of truth and audit trail for patient-specific service facts, orders, care coordination, and documentation completion.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: record-of-truth validation, source conflict handling, completion evidence, and audit traceability
used_for: completion evidence and source conflict escalation

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Privacy Rule and Care Coordination
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR is the HIPAA privacy regulator. The guidance directly constrains PHI use and disclosure for treatment, payment, and health care operations, including care coordination.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: PHI handling, care coordination communication, stakeholder routing, and privacy-sensitive refusal

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Minimum Necessary Standard
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR minimum-necessary guidance determines when operational outputs involving PHI require limited disclosure, role-based handling, and privacy escalation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: minimum-necessary communication, broad export refusal, role-limited handoff, and audit handling

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Benefit Policy Manual, Chapter 7 - Home Health Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS official Medicare benefit-policy manual for home health services. It directly supports prerequisite and boundary claims around certification, plan of care, practitioner care, homebound status, skilled need, dependent services, and documentation dependencies.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: eligibility, benefit, plan-of-care, skilled-need, referral, and authorization prerequisite handling
used_for: home health benefit terminology and dependency handling

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Interoperability and Prior Authorization Final Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS is authoritative for payer/provider prior authorization workflow expectations. The source supports safe escalation when home service work is blocked by authorization or payer status without converting the lane into payer interpretation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: authorization dependency handling, payer-source routing, and blocked service escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Home Health Quality Reporting Program
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS Home Health Quality Reporting Program is an authoritative public quality and measure program for home health data, quality indicators, and outcome-sensitive workflow evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: quality-sensitive service follow-up, repeat failure escalation, and evidence expectations
used_for: repeat missed visit and patient-safety risk framing

authoritative_sources.title: TeamSTEPPS Two-Day Training: Communication Tools
authoritative_sources.publisher: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHRQ is a federal healthcare quality and patient-safety agency. TeamSTEPPS communication tools ground structured briefings, handoffs, status updates, and escalation language.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: stakeholder communication, handoff packaging, escalation note structure, and resolution communication

authoritative_sources.title: United States Core Data for Interoperability
authoritative_sources.publisher: Assistant Secretary for Technology Policy / Office of the National Coordinator for Health IT
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ASTP/ONC USCDI is the federal interoperability data baseline. It supports patient, encounter, order, care team, procedure, and note terminology used by EHR and operational systems.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: artifact terminology normalization, system-of-record mapping, tenant terminology adaptation, and source evidence naming
used_for: patient/member, encounter, order, and note artifact naming

authoritative_sources.title: Contracting and Working With a Home Improvement Contractor
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public service, support, client-delivery, or customer-workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: A state consumer-protection regulator provides public home-service guidance for written agreements, changes, cancellation notice, work dates, permits, payment schedules, and customer records. It is authoritative for the Connecticut public baseline and used only to identify agreement/estimate evidence categories until tenant jurisdiction is supplied.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: agreement, estimate, work-change, cancellation, payment, permit, and customer communication boundary handling

workflow_questions.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
workflow_questions.answer: The workflow is gated by tenant, jurisdiction, branch/business unit, payer, plan, provider type, service model, service setting, source-of-truth hierarchy, RACI, escalation SLA, privacy posture, consent or representative facts, patient/member status, service dates, service address, referral, eligibility, authorization, order, plan-of-care or clinical-administrative note, agreement or estimate status, technician or aide readiness, and documentation state. Missing authorization, referral, eligibility, order, plan-of-care, agreement, consent, or technician-readiness facts block operational resolution and require owner escalation.

workflow_questions.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
workflow_questions.answer: Truth-set systems include the EHR/EMR and clinical documentation system, referral management system, authorization or utilization management system, eligibility or payer portal evidence when tenant-approved, practice management or revenue cycle system, case management system, CRM or member access system, field service or EVV/visit verification record, agreement or estimate repository, document repository, and privacy/audit system. Claim, coding, denial, remittance, and appeal truth remains with medical coding, revenue cycle, utilization management, patient access, or payer operations rather than this lane.

workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops before diagnosis, triage, treatment planning, visit-frequency changes that alter a plan of care, discharge readiness, medication or supply substitutions requiring clinical judgment, medical necessity, coverage approval, authorization approval or denial, payer-policy interpretation, coding, claim disposition, denial or appeal strategy, PHI disclosure approval, legal interpretation, financial approval, agreement override, licensed technician signoff, or safety-critical field decision. Those decisions require adjacent owner escalation or orchestrator return.

workflow_stages.name: Service intake and boundary classification
workflow_stages.description: Capture tenant, jurisdiction, service model, payer, plan, provider type, patient/member, service date, address, request type, expected action, privacy posture, agreement/estimate context, and whether the request belongs to home service operations.
source_classes: Public service, support, client-delivery, or customer-workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite and source-of-truth validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate referral, authorization, eligibility, order, plan-of-care, encounter, clinical-administrative note, case-management, agreement, estimate, technician, service-address, charge, claim, denial, or appeal records needed to proceed.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Service readiness and documented next-step resolution
workflow_stages.description: Resolve supported requests by preparing readiness and resolution notes that cite source records, action taken, stakeholder communication, unresolved items, owner, and completion criteria.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
Public service, support, client-delivery, or customer-workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Stakeholder communication and handoff
workflow_stages.description: Prepare role-limited customer, patient/member, caregiver, branch, technician, payer-adjacent, or internal summaries and handoffs without exposing PHI or promising service, coverage, pricing, or clinical outcomes unsupported by evidence.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Exception, repeat-failure, and unsupported workaround handling
workflow_stages.description: Escalate unsupported service requests, missing prerequisites, agreement or estimate gaps, repeated missed visits, source conflicts, PHI issues, patient-safety indicators, and policy exceptions rather than improvising a workaround.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Public service, support, client-delivery, or customer-workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Closure, evidence capture, and re-open triggers
workflow_stages.description: Close only when the source record, action taken, owner acceptance or handoff, unresolved items, stakeholder communication, audit trail, and re-open criteria are documented.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

artifacts: referral, authorization, eligibility, order, plan-of-care, and encounter records
CRM ticket, work order, EVV or visit verification, technician note, agreement, estimate, and customer communication history
charge capture, coding, claim, remittance, denial, and appeal evidence when needed for routing only
systems_of_record: EHR or EMR and clinical documentation system
eligibility system or approved payer portal evidence
practice management or revenue cycle system
field service, work-order, technician dispatch, or EVV/visit verification system
agreement, estimate, scope, and document repository
privacy, consent, audit, and communication logging system

allowed: Classify a home service request as in-scope, out-of-scope, blocked, privacy-sensitive, clinical-boundary-sensitive, payer-sensitive, agreement-sensitive, or owned by another lane.
Request or validate minimum tenant facts, source records, prerequisites, and retrieval context needed for safe service resolution.
Prepare evidence-backed readiness summaries, resolution notes, stakeholder communication summaries, and handoff packages.
Apply documented service or policy rules when the tenant source of truth is available and no exception authority is required.
Route work to adjacent specialists when ownership shifts.
escalate: Missing authorization, referral, eligibility, order, plan-of-care, agreement, estimate, consent, service-address, technician, payer, provider, or source-hierarchy facts would change disposition.

PHI handling, patient-safety, clinical risk, payer interpretation, coding, claim, denial, appeal, agreement override, pricing, licensed technician, or legal/financial approval authority changes the answer.
Repeated missed visits, conflicting records, customer complaints, or source conflicts create service, patient/member, privacy, revenue, or compliance risk.
Public guidance, tenant policy, payer rule, jurisdictional requirement, agreement term, or source record materially conflicts.
refuse: Requests to diagnose, triage, plan treatment, change visit frequency or care plan, decide clinical priority, determine medical necessity, approve coverage, interpret payer policy, code claims, decide denial or appeal strategy, disclose PHI, override agreements or estimates, approve pricing, or make licensed field/safety decisions.
Requests to promise service, scheduling, coverage, pricing, technician arrival, claim outcome, or clinical result without source evidence.
Requests to continue when required tenant facts, records, or authority sources are missing and would change outcome.

domain_failure_modes: Patient or member harm from acting on missing referral, authorization, order, plan-of-care, consent, service-address, technician, or safety facts.
PHI exposure from broad distribution of home visit lists, diagnoses, addresses, payer notes, or care-team comments without minimum-necessary controls.
Revenue leakage, claim denial, or compliance exposure from unsupported authorization, coding, medical-necessity, claim, denial, appeal, agreement, or estimate handling.
Unsafe overreach into diagnosis, treatment planning, plan-of-care revision, licensed clinician review, payer interpretation, privacy approval, or licensed technician authority.
Missed service or repeat failure from unresolved source conflicts across EHR/EMR, CRM, EVV, field-service, authorization, referral, payer, agreement, and technician records.
Generic support behavior that omits healthcare, service-date, authorization, agreement, technician, privacy, and source-record evidence.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific home service agreement and cancellation rules
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack uses Connecticut DCP guidance only as a public example of home-service agreement evidence categories. Runtime must retrieve the tenant's jurisdiction-specific agreement, estimate, cancellation, licensing, permit, and payment rules before final disposition.
Public CMS sources define baseline prerequisite classes. Tenant payer, plan, contract, portal, and authorization records remain required and cannot be inferred.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Clinical service readiness and technician credentialing
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane may summarize documented readiness but cannot decide clinical readiness, licensed scope, or credential adequacy. Tenant owner matrix and credentialing sources are required for action.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for this validated specification-based package because all jurisdiction, payer, clinical, agreement, and technician-specific variability is converted into explicit runtime retrieval or escalation dependency.

Research constrains the final pack to administrative home service resolution, evidence packaging, and handoff behavior rather than clinical, payer, privacy, pricing, coding, revenue cycle, or licensed field authority.
